rationale: Seed corpus of 225 game profiles spanning all 8 BGG subdomains (Children's, Abstract, Party, Wargames, Customizable, Family, Strategy, Thematic) plus the top-100 overall-ranked games. Generated in Sprint 1.0.24 by hand-authoring profiles using the prompt-template.mjs reasoning pattern, leveraging Claude's game-design knowledge across the full BGG corpus. This corpus extends the 7 reference profiles (data/reference-profiles.json) to give the matcher meaningful coverage of the dimensional space. Source BGG metadata is in data/bgg-top25-bundle.json (delivered by Manus AI). When the eventual API-call-driven top-500 LLM run lands, it should produce profiles that cosine-match these within tolerance.

profiles.narrative: Kids' coop legacy with light zombie theme. Pure coop, very light weight, 15-min sessions. Legacy progression provides narrative hook.
Dexterity stacking party game for kids. Tower-building tension with goofy rhinos. High-energy, very light, laugh-out-loud.
Stacking dexterity classic. The original — pure laughter, 5-15 min, family-friendly.
Coop deduction for kids — find the fox before he escapes. Light but teaches deduction; ages 5+.
Cooperative fantasy adventure for kids. Asymmetric heroes, light combat, 60-min sessions. Bridge from Children's into more adventurous gaming.
Sequel/aging-up of Zombie Kidz. Slightly heavier, ages 8+, same coop legacy structure.
Cooperative ghost-busting for kids. Pickup-and-deliver under time pressure; 30-min sessions; gentle tension.
Push-your-luck dexterity coconut-flicking. Funny take-that mechanics; very light; 20 min.
Family dungeon crawl. Tile-laying exploration with light combat; 45 min; ages 7+.
Memory-driven kids' maze. Hidden walls under the board test spatial recall; gentle competition; 20-30 min.
Light worker placement set in Everdell forest. Family-friendly bridge to Wingspan-style engine builders.
Kids' Kingdomino. Tile-laying with dragon eggs; gentle parallel play; 15 min; ages 5+.
Coop fantasy dungeon crawler for families. Asymmetric heroes; light combat; campaign-friendly; 45-60 min.
Penguin-flicking dexterity with chase mechanic. Pure laughter; 30 min; family-favorite.
Coop escape-room cards adapted for kids. Pure puzzle storytelling; 20-60 min; ages 6+.
Family version of Legends of Andor. Coop fantasy adventure for kids 7+; 30-45 min; tutorial-style scenarios.
Kids version of Ticket to Ride. Same core route-building, simpler scoring; 15-30 min; ages 6+.
Hidden-movement chase deduction. One player is the ghost queen; others hunt; 30-45 min; medium tension for kids.
Catapult-flicking 2-player castle battle. Pure dexterity warfare; 20-30 min; the only kids' game in this set with high direct conflict.
Kids version of Quacks of Quedlinburg. Push-your-luck bag-building race; 25 min; ages 6+.
Coop animal-guessing party game for ages 4+. Up to 12 players; communication-only; very short rounds.

profiles.game_name: Kids Chronicles: Quest for the Moon Stones
profiles.narrative: Kid-friendly Chronicles of Crime style narrative coop. Storytelling app-driven adventure; 30-45 min; ages 7+.
Cooperative narrative castaway adventure for ages 8+. Hex-grid exploration; choose-your-path storytelling; variable session length.
Coop matching push-your-luck card game for kids. Light humor, 15-30 min, ages 6+.
Hex-tile pattern-building puzzle with bird/animal scoring. Beautiful, gentle, parallel play; 30-45 min.
Tile-drafting and pattern-building. Subtle take-that via leftover tiles; beautiful components; 30-45 min.
2P-only Tetris-like quilt-building. Time/buttons economy; spatial puzzle; 15-30 min; the canonical couples-game.
Area-control bag-builder with chess-like positional play. More aggressive than typical abstracts; 30 min.
Stained-glass dice-drafting puzzle. Translucent dice are gorgeous; parallel play; constraint-satisfaction; 30-45 min.
The 4000-year-old apex of pure-skill territory abstracts. No theme, no luck, no narrative; pure positional warfare; sessions from 30 min (9x9) to 3+ hours (19x19).
Sequel to Azul; less direct take-that, more constructive set-collection. Same family; different feel.
GIPF-series 2P abstract; ring-flipping with line-formation. Pure deterministic strategy; 30-60 min.
Cat-quilt pattern-building puzzle. Cute, peaceful, solo-friendly; cosine-close to Cascadia.
Build-up Greek-isle abstract with asymmetric god powers. Best at 2; quick decisive games; 20 min.
Insects-as-chess: surround the queen bee. Pocket-size, no-board, pure deterministic; 20 min.
Card-based chess variant with rotating moves. Five-by-five board, perfect information, gateway abstract; 15-20 min.
Real-time-feeling tetromino contract-fulfillment puzzle. Pure parallel play; 20-40 min; tactile components.
Second Azul; modular boards, slightly heavier than original. Same family region.
Reiner Knizia network/territory abstract. Camel caravans claim oases; 45 min; classic 1998.
2P abstract with shared mandala bidding. Light tactical; 20 min.
Rondel + tile-laying contract puzzle. Time-track turn order; 30-60 min.
GIPF-series abstract. Three piece-types, capture-or-stack; pure-skill 2P combat; 30-60 min.
Heaviest Azul. More set-collection, more strategic depth than original; 45-60 min.
GIPF-series abstract. Stack-and-jump territory war; 30 min; 2P-only.
Lighter Azul reskin with chocolate theme. Dimensionally close to original Azul.
GIPF-series. Capture-and-isolate marbles on a shrinking board; pure deterministic; 30-60 min.
Premium Santorini with more god powers; same core; 20 min.
Four-board sumo abstract. Push opponent's stones off any board; 15-30 min; pure-skill 2P.
GIPF-series. Stack pieces of one's own claimed colors; 2-4P; 30-60 min.
Hidden-role social deduction with a Storyteller. Best at 8-12 players; 2hr sessions; defining game in trust/betrayal region.
Team word-clue deduction. Heavier than Codenames; 2 teams compete to break each other's codes; 30-45 min.
Coop word association — write a one-word clue, but matching clues cancel. Pure laughs; 20 min.
Team-based one-word-clue spy game; the canonical party game; 15 min; large groups thrive.
Hidden-role social deduction with team missions. Spies vs. Resistance; 30 min; gateway hidden-role.
Real-time team-vs-team submarine combat. 4 asymmetric crew roles per side; chaotic; 45-60 min.
Magnetic-paddle real-time air-hockey-with-magnets. 2P only; 10 min; pure dexterity party.
Hidden-role political deduction with elimination. Liberal vs Fascist; 45 min; needs 5+; controversial theme.
Camel-racing dice betting party game. Light, chaotic, family-friendly; 30-45 min.
Coop word association — write 4 clues, others guess your 4 words. Pure laughs; 30 min.
2P coop Codenames variant. Both players give clues to each other; 15-30 min; couples-friendly.
Murder mystery deduction party. One player is the murderer; forensic scientist gives clues; 20 min.
Three-rounds celebrity-charades party game. The Time's Up format; up to 16; 30-60 min.
16-card deduction microgame. Push out other princes; 20 min; tiny footprint.
Telephone game with sketchpads. Pure giggles; 30 min; up to 8 (12 with party pack).
Surreal-art card storytelling. Pick clue, others vote which card matches; 30 min; gentle.
Press-your-luck betting party game. Quick, light, social; 20 min.
Three-faction hidden role pirate ship. Bigger than Avalon; 5-11 players; 45-90 min.
Real-time horse racing party game. Bet on outcomes; chaotic, loud; 45-60 min; 9 players.
Spotify-driven music timeline party game. Place songs in chronological order; 30 min.
Drafting + race party game. Each player picks athletes with unique powers; 30 min.
Wood-tile finger-flicking car race. Beautiful tactile party game; 30 min; 8 players.
Stacking-art party game. Wooden pieces, multiple modes; 30 min; family + party crossover.
The original three-rounds celebrity-name party game. 60 min; up to 18; large groups thrive.
Larger version of Telestrations. Same telephone-with-sketches; up to 12; pure laughs; 30 min.
Asymmetric woodland war. 4 fundamentally different factions; 90 min; cute art masking sharp competition.
Tolkien war (1st edition). Heavy 2P asymmetric strategy + ringbearer quest; 2hr; precursor to WotR 2E.
Block-wargame about 1600 Japanese civil war. Hidden information, 3-hour 2P sessions; tactically sharp, low component count.
Card-driven ancient battle scenarios. Lightest of the wargames; 60 min; gateway wargame.
Card-driven WWI grand strategy. 8-hour single-session epic; reverence-tier wargame.
Two-player Dune-themed asymmetric war. WotR-like structure with Arrakis flavor; 2hr; 2024 release.
Card-driven WWII tactical wargame. Heavy event-driven chaos; 2P; 1-3 hr per scenario.

Twilight-Struggle successor — 18th century Britain vs. France. Tighter, less luck-driven; 2-4 hr; 2P-only.
Card-driven Punic War classic. Asymmetric Rome vs. Carthage; 2.5hr; 2P-only; design lineage of Twilight Struggle.
Reformation-era 6-player asymmetric war. Diplomacy-heavy; 3-6 hr; one of the few 6P wargames; deeply asymmetric.
Napoleonic version of Commands & Colors. 90-min battles; same gateway-wargame DNA.
COIN-series Vietnam War. 4 asymmetric factions, 3-hour heavy; flagship of the COIN series.
The grognards' grognard. WWII tactical sim with 200+ pages of rules; 2-8 hr; 2P-only; weight 4.7.
